g.text: M4 Output / Streaming Bugs Caught By
Pad of 0-byte input (buf[0]=0x80, 55 zero bytes, length=0x0000...0000). H[0..7] reset to initial constants. Single call to sha256_compress. Digest output byte ordering.
Standard single-block path. Padding: 0x61 0x62 0x63 0x80 [52 zeros] 0x18. W[0]=0x61626380. 64 compression rounds. H update. Big-endian output of H[0..7]. Idempotence of two independent calls.

CRITICAL BOUNDARY: 56 msg bytes + 0x80 = 57 bytes, only 7 bytes remain but 8 needed for length field. Forces extra block. Block-1: [56 bytes][0x80][7 zeros]. Block-2: [56 zeros][length=448 bits]. Merkle-Damgard chaining H between blocks. Loop must iterate num_blocks=2.

Empty → single-block zero-input path | abc → standard single-block | 56-byte → two-block boundary. Together: exercises all code paths in padding, schedule, compression, and output formatting. NOT exhaustive (no 1-million-'a' test, no random fuzz). Passing all 3 = strong but not complete confidence.
TV-3: W[0..15] from block-2 all-zero except length
Any: wrong sigma constants diverge immediately
TV-2: H after compress matches NIST appendix
TV-3: H after block-1 feeds into block-2 correctly
Any: swapped Ch/Maj or uppercase/lowercase sigma
TV-2 run twice: ctx reset between calls
TV-3: total_bits tracked across two update() calls
